metadata.seed: I mean, I don't. I don't know. I'm really wondering if. I don't know. I don't know if this is what I'm cut out for just thinking about. Maybe I shouldn't even bother coming in tomorrow. Teaching's my thing. Maybe I need to find another job.

patient.query: I mean, I don't. I don't know. I'm really wondering if. I don't know. I don't know if this is what I'm cut out for just thinking about. Maybe I shouldn't even bother coming in tomorrow. Teaching's my thing. Maybe I need to find another job.

automatic_thoughts: I don't know if this is what I'm cut out for
maybe I shouldn't even bother coming in tomorrow
maybe I need to find another job

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling quite uncertain about your teaching role right now. I'm hearing a lot of self-doubt in what you're saying. It feels like you're questioning whether you're suited for this position.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I am. I thought I would feel more confident by now, but every time I stand in front of the class, I just... I don't know, it's like I freeze up. It makes me wonder if I really belong here.

triggers: standing in front of the class
automatic_thoughts: I thought I would feel more confident
I freeze up
I wonder if I really belong here

entailment: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the anxiety you're feeling is really intense, especially when you're in that situation. I wonder if you've ever considered that your sense of belonging might not be entirely dependent on how confident you feel in those moments?

patient.query: Maybe. I mean, I know I’ve worked hard to get where I am, but then I just think about all the little mistakes I make and how they seem to overshadow everything else. It’s like those moments of doubt just take over, and I feel so out of place.

automatic_thoughts: little mistakes overshadow everything else
moments of doubt take over

llm_response.response: It sounds like those doubts are really weighing on you, and it must be difficult to feel like your small mistakes are defining your whole experience. I can see how that would create a lot of internal conflict and make you feel disconnected from your progress.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s frustrating. I keep telling myself that everyone makes mistakes, but when I’m up there, it just feels like I’m the only one messing up. It’s like I’m constantly comparing myself to others, and that just makes it worse. I don’t want to be this way, but it’s hard to shake off.

automatic_thoughts: I'm the only one messing up
everyone makes mistakes

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the pressure to be perfect is really weighing on you, especially when you're in those moments where you feel like you're the only one failing. Can you tell me more about how that feeling of comparison affects you emotionally?

patient.query: It definitely makes me feel... I guess not good enough? It’s like a wave of anxiety just hits me, and I start second-guessing everything. Sometimes I wonder if I’m really cut out for this, you know? It just makes me feel really small and overwhelmed.

automatic_thoughts: not good enough
second-guessing everything
wonder if I'm really cut out for this
feel really small

llm_response.response: "It sounds like those feelings of being 'not good enough' are really unsettling for you, and they seem to bring up a lot of anxiety and overwhelm. It makes sense that you'd feel that way when you're questioning things so much. Can you tell me more about what it feels like when that happens?"

patient.query: Yeah, it’s really unsettling. When I start to feel that way, my heart races, and my mind just goes blank. I feel like everyone is watching and judging me, and I can’t think straight. It’s almost like I just want to disappear because the pressure feels so heavy.

automatic_thoughts: everyone is watching and judging me
I can’t think straight
I just want to disappear
the pressure feels so heavy

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of physical and emotional distress right now. The racing heart and feeling overwhelmed must be incredibly difficult. It's understandable that your mind feels foggy during these moments."

patient.query: It really is tough. Sometimes I just wish I could be more relaxed or focused, but it’s like my body has a mind of its own. I hear you, and it helps to know that someone understands how hard it is, but it’s still frustrating to feel trapped like this.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I could be more relaxed
I wish I could be more focused
My body has a mind of its own
I feel trapped

llm_response.response: I hear how overwhelming it feels to be stuck in your own mind, especially when you're struggling to find calm or focus. It sounds like you're carrying a lot of frustration right now.

patient.query: It really is tough. I just wish I could be more relaxed or focused, but it feels like my body has a mind of its own. It's frustrating to feel trapped like this. Sometimes I just wish I could disappear because the pressure feels so heavy.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I could be more relaxed
I wish I could be more focused
My body has a mind of its own
I feel trapped
I wish I could disappear
The pressure feels heavy

llm_response.response: I hear how overwhelming this feels for you. It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure and frustration, and those physical sensations must be really distressing. Can you tell me more about what's behind those thoughts?

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel like a lot sometimes. I think a big part of it is the fear of not meeting expectations—mine or others'. I guess I worry that if I mess up, it’ll just confirm everything I’m afraid of, like I'm not smart enough or capable enough. It’s like this loop in my head that just won’t stop.

automatic_thoughts: fear of not meeting expectations
worry about messing up
confirmation of being not smart enough
confirmation of being not capable enough

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the pressure to meet expectations is creating a cycle of worry for you. When you mentioned the "loop in your head," it makes me think those thoughts might be replaying and reinforcing the fear of not being good enough. Can you tell me more about how that feeling started or what makes it feel so persistent?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I can trace it back to when I was younger. I always felt like I had to be the best in school, and if I wasn’t, it felt like a failure. It’s just stuck with me, and now it feels like I carry that pressure into everything I do. I can’t shake off that voice telling me I need to be perfect, and it just... it feels really exhausting.

triggers: pressure to be the best in school
automatic_thoughts: I need to be perfect
I will fail if I'm not the best

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like that intense pressure to always be perfect has become a heavy burden for you, and it's clear how much emotional weight it carries. Hearing you describe that internal voice makes me think this expectation has been shaping your experiences for a long time.
